skill.name: reagent-substitute-scout
skill.description: Find validated alternative reagents based on literature citation data.
skill.summary: Search scientific literature databases to identify validated reagent alternatives with citation evidence and reliability scoring.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate scientific research tool for finding validated reagent alternatives. It makes HTTP requests to public scientific APIs (PubMed, PubChem) and reads optional API keys from user-supplied configuration files. The majority of the 94 static findings are false positives: backtick findings in SKILL.md are Markdown code formatting (342 instances), not shell execution; weak cryptographic algorithm findings point to non-cryptographic code; and system reconnaissance findings are standard data processing loops. However, the skill legitimately accesses network APIs, reads credential configs, and writes output files, which warrants a medium risk level with documentation of these capabilities.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Write Access via User-Controlled Path
medium_findings.description: The script writes output to a user-specified file path via argparse --output argument. This is standard CLI tool behavior but represents filesystem modification capability.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The output path is user-controlled via argparse, making this legitimate CLI behavior. Still represents a filesystem write capability.

medium_findings.title: Programmatic Access to API Credentials
medium_findings.description: The PubMedDataSource constructor reads api_key from configuration and passes it as a query parameter to NCBI API requests. This is necessary for PubMed API rate limit improvements.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: API key is read from config dict and used in query parameters. Legitimate for NCBI API access but represents credential exposure risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Configuration Example
low_findings.description: config.example.json contains hardcoded URLs for public scientific API endpoints (ChEMBL and PubChem). These are legitimate public API base URLs used as configuration examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to public scientific API endpoints (ebi.ac.uk, ncbi.nlm.nih.gov). Legitimate and necessary for the skill's function.

low_findings.title: Placeholder API Keys in Documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains example configuration with placeholder API key values. These are clearly fake placeholder values for documentation purposes only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Values like 'your_ncbi_api_key' are obvious documentation placeholders. Not actual credentials but could encourage users to hardcode keys.

low_findings.title: Home Directory Cache Path in Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation mentions cache directory at ~/.cache path. This is standard XDG convention for application cache data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: ~/.cache is the standard Linux XDG cache directory location. Not hidden file access in a malicious sense.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Key in HTTP Query Parameters
dangerous_patterns.description: PubMed API key is passed as an HTTP query parameter (?api_key=) in GET requests to eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov. API keys in query strings can be logged by intermediaries or exposed in referrer headers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: This is the documented NCBI API key passing mechanism, but query parameter exposure is a recognized security concern.

content.user_title: Find Validated Reagent Substitutes from Literature Data
content.value_statement: Researchers face supply chain disruptions when specific reagents are discontinued or out of stock. This skill searches scientific literature databases to identify validated alternative reagents with citation evidence and reliability scoring.
actual_capabilities: Search for alternative reagents by name, CAS number, or molecular formula across multiple literature databases
Query PubMed for citation evidence on proposed reagent alternatives
Calculate chemical structure similarity using molecular fingerprints via RDKit
Score and rank alternatives based on citation count, recency, and structural similarity
Generate structured output in table, JSON, or markdown format with literature evidence
Match reagents against a built-in offline database of common laboratory substitutes
limitations: Requires internet access for PubMed, PubChem, and ChEMBL API queries to return results
Chemical structure similarity calculation requires the RDKit library to be installed
Results depend on public database availability and completeness of literature data
Does not verify current reagent availability in local supply chains or laboratory inventory

use_cases.title: Laboratory Supply Chain Disruption
use_cases.description: A lab manager needs to find validated alternatives for a discontinued reagent to avoid experiment delays.

use_cases.title: Research Protocol Optimization
use_cases.description: A researcher explores cost-effective or higher-performance reagent alternatives for an existing protocol.

use_cases.title: Method Validation Documentation
use_cases.description: A quality assurance team requires documented evidence for reagent substitutions in validated methods.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Reagent Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for validated substitute reagents for TRIzol Reagent and provide a table with similarity scores and citation counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple reagent name lookup with default output format

prompt_templates.title: CAS Number Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Find alternative reagents with CAS number 15596-18-2. Show results in JSON format with literature evidence for each candidate.
prompt_templates.scenario: Precise chemical identification using CAS registry number

prompt_templates.title: Application-Specific Filtering
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for substitutes for TRIzol Reagent that are validated for RNA extraction. Limit results to 5 alternatives and include detailed literature citations for each candidate.
prompt_templates.scenario: Domain-specific search with application field filtering and verbose literature output

prompt_templates.title: Full Configuration and Custom Scoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure the reagent-substitute-scout with custom scoring weights: citation_weight 0.3, recency_weight 0.4, similarity_weight 0.3. Search for alternatives to Fetal Bovine Serum and generate a markdown report with all available literature evidence.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced usage with custom scoring parameters and markdown report generation

output_examples.input: Find substitutes for TRIzol Reagent and show a table
Search for alternatives to DMSO with literature evidence
output: Found 2 validated substitutes for Dimethyl Sulfoxide:
2. Dimethyl Sulfoxide itself (CAS: 67-68-5) - Similarity: 1.0, Citations: 5, Reliability: ★★

best_practices: Always verify suggested alternative reagents against your specific experimental conditions before use
Use CAS numbers rather than common names for more precise reagent identification and matching
Cross-reference results from multiple data sources to confirm the validity of alternatives
anti_patterns: Do not rely solely on similarity scores without considering application-specific validation data
Do not use the skill without internet connectivity since it requires access to external API databases
Do not assume all listed alternatives are in current production or available from suppliers

faq.question: What databases does this skill search for reagent alternatives?
faq.answer: The skill searches PubMed for literature citations, PubChem for chemical structure data, and ChEMBL for bioactivity information. It also includes an offline database of common laboratory reagent substitutes.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: PubMed and other NCBI databases can be accessed without an API key, but providing one allows higher request rate limits. PubChem and ChEMBL do not require API keys.

faq.question: How are alternatives ranked and scored?
faq.answer: Alternatives are scored using three weighted dimensions: citation count evidence, recency of publications, and chemical structure similarity. The default weights are 0.4 for citations, 0.3 for recency, and 0.3 for similarity.

faq.question: Can this skill verify if a reagent is available from suppliers?
faq.answer: No. The skill identifies scientifically validated alternatives from literature data but does not check current supplier inventory or pricing.

faq.question: What is the offline database and how is it used?
faq.answer: The offline database contains pre-compiled substitute mappings for common reagents such as TRIzol, DMSO, and FBS. It provides quick results without API calls but covers only a limited set of reagents.

faq.question: Can I customize the scoring weights?
faq.answer: Yes. The configuration file allows you to adjust citation_weight, recency_weight, and similarity_weight to prioritize different aspects of the evaluation for your specific needs.
